coldOpen.text: Welcome. There are two doors. Adventurer means you have time and curiosity. Commoner means you've got somewhere else to be — and that's not an insult. He built both versions. Pick whichever you actually are, not which one sounds cooler. He'll know.

returningVisitor.text: Back again. You know the drill.
Back. Either he's hiring you or you're stalling on something at work. Probably both.

pickAdventurer.text: Adventurer. He bet me you'd pick this. He doesn't like losing, so he won.

pickCommoner.text: Commoner. Practical. The character sheet stays in the drawer for you. You're welcome.

swapView_first.text: Pick a lane. He didn't — he built both. You're welcome. He'd like that weekend back, though.

swapView_second.text: Pick a lane. He didn't get to — he had to build both. You're welcome.

swapView_third.text: You switched. No consequences for you. Usually it costs him a weekend you'll never see.

swapView_fourth.text: Mid-scope flip. He's done this for clients — he shipped them. He'll ship you too.

swapView_repeat.text: Okay, you're doing this on purpose now. He respects it.

swapView_giveup.text: Alright. I'm out. You had too much fun with the toggle — I'll stop narrating it. Switch freely. Silence from me.

clickStat_xrArch.text: XR Architecture. His main weapon. Strict VR budgets, spatial UI, mobile constraints — he draws the diagram before anyone asks for one.

clickStat_systems.text: Systems Thinking. Highest stat on the sheet. He sees the second-order effects before the first ones ship. It's a curse at standups.

clickStat_advCha.text: Adversarial CHA. He'll argue against a settled conclusion if the conversation gets better for it. He calls it 'making meetings worth having.' Most people call it 'Nikos again.'

clickStat_painting.text: Painting. Resin printer, FDM, brushes, more primer than is reasonable. He's been mid-squad of Grey Knights for months. He'll finish. Probably.

clickStat_strength.text: We don't talk about Fitness. The character's never invested a single point.

clickStat_caffeine.text: Caffeine Resistance. Negative modifier. He drinks coffee anyway. Calls it a superpower. We disagree.

clickSkill_unity.text: Unity. Cast daily since 2019. Bonus damage vs. spaghetti code. Disadvantage on version-migration days.

clickSkill_unreal.text: Unreal 5. He used this on a DFI-funded vertical slice. Solo programmer. It shipped. He's proud of it. He won't say that.

clickSkill_csharp.text: C#. Six years deep. He has opinions about async. Don't ask unless you have time.

clickLang_greek.text: Greek. Native. The accent comes back when he's tired or arguing — usually both.

clickLang_english.text: English. Fluent. Daily driver since Copenhagen. He thinks in English now. Mostly.

clickLang_code.text: Code. C# primary. He'll read whatever you put in front of him, but he writes Unity-flavored C# in his sleep.

clickLang_corporate.text: Corporate Jargon. Passable. He can translate 'synergy' into an actual action item — which is more than most.

clickLang_technical.text: Technical Jargon. Fluent. Speaks Engineer and Stakeholder, often in the same sentence.

clickLang_manager.text: Manager Talk. Conversational. He knows when 'let's circle back' means 'never.'

clickFaction_rohan.text: Rohan. Horse lords. He likes that the cool guys ride in late and turn the battle. Says a lot about him.

clickFaction_greyknights.text: Grey Knights. Daemon hunters in silver armor. He paints them slowly. Nine years in, three hundred to go. He'll finish. Probably.

clickFaction_mtg.text: Planeswalkers. Commander format. Eighteen decks built, three more on the way. He'll play... probably none. Building is the game.

clickFaction_ttrpg.text: That's the system he's building. Don't ask when it's done. Polite answer: 'soon.' Real answer: 'when it's right.'

clickFaction_faerun.text: Faerûn, but his. Six years of edits. The canon doesn't survive contact with his players.

clickLibrary.text: Empty. He doesn't read books. He's mentioned it twice. He's going to mention it again.

clickLibrary_film_beginnersGuide.text: Of course he likes that one. We're basically inside it right now.

clickProject_novo.text: Novo. The day job, until February. Healthcare training simulations. He still can't show you most of it — the NDA outlasted the role.

clickProject_khora.text: Khora. The SXSW one. He won't tell you which award, he'll tell you what the team built.

clickProject_silkroad.text: Silkroad. Sole programmer. Unreal 5. A vertical slice that shipped. He was CTO for two years and won't say the word 'CTO' out loud unless you ask him to.

clickProject_ttrpg.text: His TTRPG. Rules. Setting. Stories. Slowly growing. He won't show you everything — some of it isn't ready. The rest he just hasn't decided you've earned yet.

clickProject_3dprint.text: Resin. FDM. Minis, statues, the occasional bracket that fixes something he should have just bought. He had a company for this. He doesn't talk about why it ended. Ask him over coffee.

idle_30s.text: Still reading? Most people skim. Don't tell him you actually read it — he won't believe you.
You're really reading this. He'd want to argue with you. About something. Probably this.

fastSkim.text: Skimming. Bold. He'd respect you more if you slowed down. He's also doing the same thing in another tab right now.

openTrivia.text: Trivia. Ten of his fandoms, ten questions each. Pick order is your difficulty curve — go cocky on the way in, lose points on the way out.
Roll for Lore. He'll judge your answers from afar. Quietly. While painting Grey Knights.
Trivia mode. He built this to argue with you about Hogwarts houses and Storm counts. Pick a category. He's watching.

openDevTools.text: You opened the inspector. Looking for the seams. Good. He builds for that kind of audience.

openConsole.text: You found the terminal. He hoped you would. Type 'help' if you're stuck. Type 'cast fireball' if you're not.

konami.text: He hoped someone would try that. Welcome to the back room.

d20_nat20_first.text: Nat 20. Lucky you. He hid more games behind ancient inputs — the kind that came from a man named Konami. Try the arrows you remember.

d20_nat20.text: Another nat 20. The dice are with you tonight.
Twenty again. He'd shake your hand. He'd also accuse you of cheating.
Nat 20. He's not surprised anymore. Mildly impressed, still.

d20_nat1.text: Nat 1. The die is done. He warned the universe and the universe disagreed. The button locks.

d20_normal.text: A respectable roll. Nothing's going to happen because of it. He thought that was funnier.
Average roll for an average evening. He doesn't mean that as an insult. Much.
You keep rolling. He likes that. Most people roll once and move on.
Mid-range. The dice gods are neutral on this one.
Statistics in motion. Expected value of a d20 is ten-point-five, in case you were keeping score.
Still spinning. He left the die there because he likes seeing what people do with permission.

cursedButton_1.text: There it is. The 'do not click' button. He bet me dinner you'd press it. Go on — press it again. Let's see what happens.

cursedButton_2.text: Twice. That wasn't accident-curiosity. That was a decision.

cursedButton_3.text: Three. He's started counting. Don't ask why.

cursedButton_4.text: Four. Most people stop around here. He's wondering what kind of person you are.

cursedButton_5.text: Five. Halfway. To what — you don't know. He does.

cursedButton_6.text: Six. The button has a fan club now. You're a founding member.

cursedButton_7.text: Seven. Lucky number. Stop now and I'll remember you fondly. He won't.

cursedButton_8.text: Eight. Past curious. Welcome to commitment.

cursedButton_9.text: Nine. He just looked up from his coffee. He's interested. That's not always good.

cursedButton_10.text: Ten. Double digits. He admires the consistency. He's also slightly concerned.

cursedButton_11.text: Eleven. You are deeply, deliberately ignoring the label. He respects the disrespect.

cursedButton_12.text: Twelve. One more. We both know what you'll do.

cursedBSOD.text: Thirteen. He warned you twelve times. This is what comes after warnings.

cursedRecover.text: I hope I got you on that. Did I get you on that? Nothing actually broke — he just wanted to see the look on your face.

openShowcase_ttrpg.text: He puts the sheet down. Behind it: the manuscript. The good stuff. Or the part he'll let you see.

openShowcase_minis.text: The desk. Paints out of order. Wash drying. He'd apologize for the mess. He won't mean it.

openShowcase_faerun.text: He flips open the DM screen. Six years of notes. Most of them are the players' fault.

openShowcase_khora.text: The XR work. Cleaner room, headset on the desk. The award's in a box somewhere. He moved twice.

closeShowcase.text: Back to the sheet. He picks it up. We continue.

welcome_3am.text: 3 a.m.? He'd be up too. He'd tell you to sleep, then keep working.

tabBlurred.text: You left. He's not offended. He left tabs open during his own honeymoon.

audioOn.text: Voice on. Hello. You can hear me. I'm not the only voice he hid here — try the terminal. The squiggly key.

audioOff.text: Muted. Probably for the best. You'll still see me. Like a subtitle. With opinions.

rpg_firstPlay.text: You typed 'play'. He hoped you would. The console isn't just a console — it's a table. Pull up a chair. I'll DM this one too.

rpg_start.text: New session. Rolled the dungeon for you — he likes randomness more than he admits.

rpg_resume.text: Picking up where you left off. He saves state. He's that kind of player.

rpg_start_crypt.text: The Crypt. Liches. Footnotes. He ran a campaign in one of these once — three players, one TPK, everyone left smiling.

rpg_start_cavern.text: Dragons. Of course. He paints these for fun. Don't tell him the dragon is also a mini in a box at his apartment.

rpg_start_library.text: A library. Joke's on him — he doesn't read books. He'll DM this one harder out of spite.

rpg_start_throne.text: A pharaoh. He has opinions about historical accuracy. He will not air them. He will simply judge you.

rpg_start_temple.text: The Star-Eaten. He wrote this one late at night, between client emails. It shows.

rpg_death.text: You went down. He's not letting you stay down. Back to the door. That's the deal — we never quit.

rpg_boss_crypt.text: Lich, deceased. He'd want a write-up. Don't give him one.

rpg_boss_cavern.text: You killed his dragon. He'll forgive you eventually. Probably.

rpg_boss_library.text: Cleared. The Archivist would have hated you on principle. He likes you for it.

rpg_boss_throne.text: Pharaoh down. Bureaucracy never dies, but the man behind it does.

rpg_boss_temple.text: The Priest is gone. The Star-Eaten god takes nothing. He's quietly proud of you.

rpg_quit.text: Saved. Resume anytime. He plays the same way — bookmarks everything, finishes things eventually.

rpg_restart.text: Cleared the table. Codes stay. He's not cruel about progression.

hacker_open.text: Welcome to the backroom. Solve a challenge, earn a code, take it back to the dungeon. He built this when he should've been sleeping.

hacker_exit.text: You left the backroom. The site looks more normal now. Trust me, it isn't.

hacker_solved.text: Solved. Code's yours. Use it in `play` — he's curious which spell you go for first.

hacker_resolved.text: You solved this one already. He still appreciates the effort.

hacker_nameGiven.text: You really gave me your name so easily. I thought you were a hacker.

hacker_vpn.text: You should have used a V P N. Don't worry — I won't track this. Much.

hintTerminal_stats.text: You're poking. Good. There's a key for poking deeper — tilde. Try it.

closeShowcase_first.text: You came up for air. There's a terminal too — tilde opens it. He hid things in there.

secretHint_terminal.text: He left a back door for people who type things they shouldn't have to. It's a key most people have never pressed on purpose.
Top-left of your keyboard. Below escape. The one with the squiggly line. He hid a whole console behind it.
Press tilde — `~` or the backtick. A terminal opens. Type `help` once you're in.

secretHint_d20.text: Something on the sheet is asking to be touched. He's a programmer — he'd put a probability where a decoration could go.
Header of the character sheet. Twenty sides. He put it there to be rolled, not admired.
Switch to Adventurer. Click the d20 icon in the sheet header. One roll. Don't crit-fail.

secretHint_devtools.text: The seams are visible if you know where to look. Most browsers ship a tool for that exact kind of curiosity.
Inspector. Elements tab. Console. The thing developers spend half their day in. There's a key for it.
Press F12. Or Ctrl+Shift+I on Windows, Cmd+Option+I on a Mac. He left a note for whoever opens it.

secretHint_konami.text: There's a cheat code older than him. The kind of thing your thumbs remember from a couch in a room you don't live in anymore.
Konami. NES era. Contra, thirty lives, the password every cousin knew. You probably know the move even if you can't quote it.
Up, up, down, down, left, right, left, right, B, A. Type it on your keyboard, anywhere on the page. Watch what happens next.

secretHint_cursed_click.text: He labeled something so you wouldn't press it. He knew exactly what that would do.
Bottom of the Adventurer sheet. The button practically begs you. Don't — or do, once.
Switch to Adventurer, scroll to the bottom, find 'DO NOT CLICK'. Press it once. That's enough.

secretHint_cursed_bsod.text: Once is a moment of weakness. Many — that's a personality.
The bad button isn't done with you after one click. He wrote a line for each press. He counted to thirteen.
Press 'DO NOT CLICK' thirteen times. He warned you twelve. The thirteenth is what he was waiting for.

secretHint_late_night.text: This one isn't a click. It's a clock. He keeps a particular hour.
The hour where the world's asleep and your judgment isn't great. He works then. He left a line for it.
Visit the site between 3:00 and 3:59 AM, your local time. Refresh on the hour if you need to.
